Wood stakes lack bark and have a higher surface area to volume ratio than occurs with natural dead wood. Additionally, "naturally occurring" branches and boles may have a higher incidence of fungal colonization, due to necrotrophy being a larger driver of tree mortality in in-tact forests compared to harvested systems [@yang2022]. These differences could result in even greater ecological differences between decay patterns in in-tact vs. disturbed forest systems than we can detect in our standardized substrate study. 


# Conclusions


Our study found evidence that harvesting results in complex interacting controls on the decay of dead wood which differ from mature, harvest origin and young, mature fire-disturbed stands and that biomass removal intensification caused additional alterations to decomposition processes. Generally, harvesting causes increased initial decomposition, reducing the duration of early decay states of wood compared to natural reference conditions. Additional intensification of harvesting reduced decomposition, but in ways that were mechanistically distinct from reference conditions (i.e., they were not replicating natural or late-stage harvest recovery conditions, rather, introducing a conflicting disturbance). These results imply that harvesting fundamentally changes site characteristics in such a way that local ecological processes are modified during the regeneration stage of stand development. Our results suggest that the influence of biomass removals on microclimate conditions and biodiversity have more control over local decay and nutrient dynamics than total woody biomass stocks and result in treatment-specific alterations to decay dynamics. These changes are likely influencing the decomposition of other litter types, as well as biological activity within soils that requires more study. It would be informative to repeat this wood stake decay experiment as the regenerating stands (harvested and recently burned) advance to the crown closure stage of stand development, to determine if these patterns are maintained at a different stage of post-disturbance recovery. Ecosystem management should focus on maintaining or restoring forest structure, microclimate and decomposer community functions via retention of diverse organic sources and in-tact soils. Results from S and Blading practices demonstrated that there is additional risk to ecosystem function related to the additional soil disturbance and biomass removals. Forest management decisions should treat increased soil disturbance and below-ground biomass removal as higher risk activities and balance them appropriately against benefits. Additionally, there was minimal difference between SO and FT, so these treatments can most likely be treated as functionally equivalent. As discussed in the previous section, these functional differences mean that managers modelling deadwood availability would require separate decomposition rate estimates to account for changing forest and harvest conditions and their interactions.



# Declarations
 

## Declaration of Generative AI use


During the preparation of this work the author(s) used Government of Canada Copilot powered by GPT-5 in order to identify redundancies in writing and potential options for streamlining discussion. After using this tool/service, the author(s) reviewed options and used the suggestions as a basis for rewriting the content as needed and take(s) full responsibility for the content of the published article.
 
 
## Data Accessibility


All data generated or analysed during this study are included in this published article [and its supplementary information files]. The datasets and scripts for the current study are also available on our github repository, [https://github.com/GLFC-WET/IslandLake_WoodStakeDecomposition](https://github.com/GLFC-WET/IslandLake_WoodStakeDecomposition).


## Funding


Funding for this work was provided by the Canadian Forest Service of Natural Resources Canada. We also thank the Ontario Ministry of Natural Resources – Forest Industry Division, and Ontario Power Generation for funding that supported the establishment of the biomass harvest trial.


## Authors' contributions


ES analyzed and interpreted the datasets, drafted and edited the text and created all figures and tables. EO provided extensive editing and internal review. CE contributed to study design and field methods planning and field work. KW-K performed all wood stake installations, collections and processing. EE, DK provided lab resources and expertise as well as editing and internal review. LV designed the study, acquired the main funding and provided analytical guidance and editing throughout the writing process. 


## Acknowledgements

 
We would like to specially acknowledge the contributions of Kerrie Waino-Keizer for processing these samples within her own home during the COVID lockdowns of 2020 and 2021. The Island Lake Biomass Research and Demonstration Area represents an ongoing collaboration between the Canadian Forest Service, Ontario Ministry of Natural Resources (OMNR), and university researchers. This project was made possible with support from regional partners including David Repath and Mike Martineau from Tembec (now GreenFirst Forest Products) and Gaston and Michel Demers from Demers Brothers Logging who carefully harvested the site. We also thank local First Nations (Chapleau Cree First Nation, Northeast Superior Regional Chiefs Forum) for their support including Colin Lachance, Pat Tangie, and our past Forestry Indigenous Interns: Isabell Souliere and Jamie Bananish. We also thank Clara Laziere from Northeast Superior Forest Community and Al Stinson from the Forest Research Partnership and the Island Lake Steering Committee including Rob Fleming, Isabelle Aubin, and Kara Webster. We also thank Dr. Paul Hazlett (retired) for his integral part in the design and establishment of the Island Lake Biomass Research and Demonstration Area, as well as all the scientists, students and technical staff involved in its design, monitoring, sampling and maintenance of the site.


# References


::: {#refs}
:::


# Tables

```{r harv-ortho-table-out}

harvest.ortho.table <- data.frame(c(Type = "Harvesting-Reference comparison", Code = "TR", Contrast = "Uncut mid-aged reference vs Stem-Only and Full-treeLINEBREAK(UC vs SO & FT)", Tests = "Effect of recent harvest disturbance", Description="Comparing operational harvest of tree biomass removal to unharvested reference stands to isolates the influence of recent tree removal."),
                                 c(Type = "Harvesting-Reference comparison", Code = "OC", Contrast = "Old reference vs Stem-Only and Full-treeLINEBREAK(Old vs SO & FT)", Tests = "Comparison of harvesting to 95 year-old fire origin site", Description="Comparing the old, wildfire reference to the young regenerating stands after being harvested with the operationally approved biomass harvest methods."),
                                  c(Type = "Harvesting-Reference comparison",Code = "HF", Contrast = "Fire reference vs Stem-Only and Full-treeLINEBREAK(Fire vs SO & FT)", Tests = "Comparison of recent harvesting to recent wildfire disturbance", Description="This comparison assesses whether the operationally approved biomass removal treatments are comparable to decay processes after wildfire."), 
                                  c(Type = "Biomass removal intensification", Code = "FWD", Contrast = "Stem-Only vs Full-TreeLINEBREAK(SO vs FT)", Tests = "Effect of fine and coarse woody debris removal vs only coarse wood", Description="Comparing only the full-tree and stem-only removals to isolate the influence of removals of tree tops and branch materials compared to retention of these materials on site."),
                                  c(Type = "Biomass removal intensification", Code = "BGBR", Contrast = "Stem-Only and Full-Tree vs StumpedLINEBREAK(SO & FT vs S)", Tests = "Effect of below-ground woody biomass removal", Description="Comparing both stem-only and full-tree harvesting (approved operational harvest methods) to additional stump removal to explore the additive effect of belowground woody biomass removal."),
                                  c(Type = "Biomass removal intensification", Code = "FFR", Contrast = "Stem-Only, Full-tree and Stumped vs BladedLINEBREAK(SO & FT & S vs B)", Tests = "Effect of forest floor removal", Description="Forest floor removal is contrasted against the other tree removal approaches, as operationally employed approaches are likely to still result in a patchy mix of mineral exposure.")) %>% t()

rownames(harvest.ortho.table) <- NULL

knitr::kable(harvest.ortho.table, caption= "Orthogonal contrasts used to test effects of harvesting and biomass removal intensity on decay dynamics compared to reference conditions.")
```


# Figures

```{r site-map, fig.cap= "Map of the Island Lake Biomass Harvest Research and Demonstration Area, modified from Venier et al, (2017) Figure 2.4. For the biomass removal plots, the numbers indicate the block ID and the letters indicate the harvest treatment: SO = Stem Only, FT = Full-tree, S = Stump removal, B = Bladed, UC = Mid-aged Reference. The location of the sites in Ontario is provided in a map in the upper left corner.", fig.width= 8}
## include experimental plot design fig from ILBMB report
knitr::include_graphics("site_map.jpg", dpi = 300)

```


```{r ML-plot-controls, fig.width = 10.5, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Patterns of above- and below-ground wood stake mass loss over time for harvest-reference contrasts. Results from a mixed model are reported in panels A-C and E-G as the significance and estimates (p < 0.05) of the polynomial slopes of mass loss over time each contrast. Significant (p < 0.05) Year-based effects of each contrast, created using orthogonal contrasts applied to each year independently, are indicated with red * asterix in each panel. Boxplots represent the different levels of each contrast using colour (coloured and grey) and boxplots show the median mass loss as a central line, two hinges at the 25th and 75th percentiles and, whiskers representing 1.5*IQR. Panels D and H have points showing the decay rate (mass loss year ^-1^) for each treatment, with 95% confidence error bars, and lines displaying the trends over time, as calculated from the overall 2nd order mixed model. Treatment codes are as follows: SO = Stem-Only Harvesting, FT = Full-tree harvesting, UC = Uncut mid-age plantation, OLD = 95 year-old plantation, FIRE = Young fire disturbed plantation."}

Treatments_labels <- ggplot(data.frame(position = c(6:1), name = c("UC = 40 year unharvested", "SO = Stem-Only harvesting", "FT = Full-tree harvesting", "S = stumped", "Old = Nimitz 95 year-old control", "Fire = Ripple Lake Fire")), aes(x = 1, y = position)) + 
                                  geom_text(aes(label = name)) +
                                  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), plot.background = element_blank(), panel.background = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(), axis.title = element_blank()) 

plot_list = list(aboveground.plotlist.ML[[2]], aboveground.plotlist.ML[[3]], aboveground.plotlist.ML[[4]], belowground.plotlist.ML[[2]], belowground.plotlist.ML[[3]], belowground.plotlist.ML[[4]])

plot_list2 = list(slope_plots_ML[["H.A"]], slope_plots_ML[["H.B"]])

ggarrange(
      ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list, nrow =2, ncol = 3, labels = c("A", "B", "C", "E", "F", "G")) %>% 
            annotate_figure(left = "Mass Loss (proportion of original weight lost)"), 
      ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list2, nrow = 2, ncol = 1, labels=c("D", "H"), common.legend = T, legend = "right") %>% 
            annotate_figure(left = "Proportion original weight lost per year"), 
      ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 0.5))


```


```{r ML-plot-harv-comparisons, fig.width = 10.5, fig.height=8, fig.cap="Patterns of above- and below-ground wood stake mass loss over time for biomass intensification contrasts. Results from a mixed model are reported in panels A-C and E-G as the significance and estimates (p < 0.05) of the polynomial slopes of mass loss over time each contrast. Significant (p < 0.05) Year-based effects of each contrast, created using orthogonal contrasts applied to each year independently, are indicated with red * asterix in each panel. Boxplots represent the different levels of each contrast using colour (coloured and grey) and boxplots show the median mass loss as a central line, two hinges at the 25th and 75th percentiles and, whiskers representing 1.5*IQR. Panels D and H have points showing the decay rate (mass loss year ^-1^) for each treatment, with 95% confidence error bars, and lines displaying the trends over time, as calculated from the overall 2nd order mixed model. Treatment codes are as follows: SO = Stem-Only Harvesting, FT = Full-tree harvesting, S = FT + Stump removal, B = Blading."}

Treatments_labels <- ggplot(data.frame(position = c(4:1), name = c("SO = Stem-Only harvesting", "FT = Full-tree harvesting", "S = stumped", "B = Bladed")), aes(x = 1, y = position)) + 
                                  geom_text(aes(label = name)) +
                                  theme(axis.text = element_blank(), plot.background = element_blank(), panel.background = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(), axis.title = element_blank()) 

plot_list = list(aboveground.plotlist.ML[[5]], aboveground.plotlist.ML[[6]], aboveground.plotlist.ML[[7]], belowground.plotlist.ML[[5]], belowground.plotlist.ML[[6]], belowground.plotlist.ML[[7]])

plot_list2 = list(slope_plots_ML[["B.A"]], slope_plots_ML[["B.B"]])

ggarrange(
      ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list, nrow =2, ncol = 3, labels = c("A", "B", "C", "E", "F", "G")) %>% 
            annotate_figure(left = "Mass Loss (proportion of original weight lost)"), 
      ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list2, nrow = 2, ncol = 1, labels=c("D", "H"), common.legend = T, legend = "right") %>% 
            annotate_figure(left = "Proportion original weight lost per year)"), 
      ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 0.5))

rm(list = ls()[!ls() %in% keep.list])
```


```{r chem-summary-harvests, fig.width=11, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Orthogonal contrast results of mixed effects model tests for differences in the amount of chemical remaining in decaying stakes incubated above and below-ground for tested harvesting contrasts (TR = Harvested/Mid-aged reference, OC = Harvested/old reference, HF = Harvested/recent fire contrast). Each chemical compound is displayed as text at the effect size based on glm mixed models including a polynomial Mass loss relationship, orthogonal contrasts, and field replicate as a random effect. Error bars display the standard error of the estimated effect size. The treatments in the contrast are displayed on the graph to indicate the direction of effect. Significant effects are indicated with dark grey if they indicate increased values in harvested treatments, contrast-related coloration if they are increased in the reference conditions, and insignificant effects are indicated in light grey. "}
Chem_4plot %>% 
      group_by(Contrast, name, position, Estimate, `Std. Error`, `Pr(>|t|)`) %>%
      mutate(Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("TR", "FWD", "BGBR", "FFR","OC", "HF"))) %>%
      filter(Contrast %in% c("TR", "OC", "HF"), !name %in% c("C", "CNRatio")) %>%
      summarize(groupA = unique(group)[1], 
                groupB = unique(group)[2]) %>% 
      filter(!is.na(Contrast)) %>% 
      mutate(significant = ifelse(`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05, 
                                  ifelse(Estimate > 0, "pos", "neg"), 
                                  "ns")) %>%
      group_by(name) %>% 
      mutate(has.sig = sum(`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05) > 0) %>%
      mutate(position = ifelse(position == "Above-ground", "Above", "Below")) %>%
      mutate(name = str_replace_all(name, c("Aluminum" = "Al", "Boron" = "B", "Calcium" = "Ca", "Copper" = "Cu", "Iron" = "Fe", "Magnesium" = "Mg", "Manganese" = "Mn", "Phosphorus" = "P", "Potassium" = "K", "Zinc" = "Zn", "CNRatio" = "C/N"))) %>%
      mutate(category = ifelse(name %in% c("C", "N", "P", "C/N", "K", "Ca", "Mg"), "macronutrient", ifelse(name %in% c("Fe", "Al", "Cu"), "enzyme biocatalysts", "micronutrient")) ) %>%
      group_by(position, category) %>%
      mutate(groupB.textloc=max(Estimate + `Std. Error`)*1.1, 
             groupA.textloc=min(Estimate - `Std. Error`)*1.1) %>% group_by() %>% 
      mutate(plotting_group = paste(position, Contrast, category), 
             plot_title = paste(Contrast, position)) %>% 
      arrange(category, position, Contrast)-> chem.effect.plot

excluded.vars <- c(unique(chem.effect.plot$name[!chem.effect.plot$has.sig]))

plot_list <- list()
count = 1
for(plot_group in unique(chem.effect.plot$plotting_group)) {
      
      title_text <- chem.effect.plot %>% filter(has.sig, plotting_group == plot_group) %>% summarize(unique(plot_title)) %>% unlist()
      
         plot_panel_colors <- c(TR = "black", FWD = "#44AA99", BGBR = "#CC6677", FFR = "#882235", OC = "#117733", HF = "#AA4499", ML = "black")
      plot_panel_color <- plot_panel_colors[str_extract(title_text, str_c(names(plot_panel_colors), collapse = "|"))]
      
      plot_list[[count]] <- ggplot(chem.effect.plot %>% filter(has.sig, plotting_group == plot_group), aes(1, Estimate,  group = name, color = significant)) + 
      geom_text(aes(label=groupA, y = groupA.textloc), color = plot_panel_color, size = 5) + 
      geom_text(aes(label=groupB, y = groupB.textloc), color = "grey30", size = 5) +
      geom_hline(yintercept=0, color = "grey66", linewidth = 1) +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - `Std. Error`, ymax = Estimate + `Std. Error`), position = position_dodge(width = 1), linewidth =1) +
      geom_label(aes(label = name), position = position_dodge(width = 1)) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position="none", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.title = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey66"), plot.title = element_text(color = plot_panel_color)) + 
      scale_color_manual(values = c("ns"="grey80", "pos"="grey30", "neg" = plot_panel_color[[1]])) + 
            ggtitle(title_text)
      
      names(plot_list)[count] <- plot_group
      count = count + 1
}
rm(count, title_text)
      
      ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list, labels = "AUTO", ncol = 6, nrow = 3, legend = FALSE) %>% annotate_figure(left ="Estimated effect of contrast on the proportion of chemical amount change", bottom = paste0(str_c(excluded.vars, collapse = ", "), "  were not significantly different in any contrast."))

```


```{r chem-summary-biomass, fig.width=11, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Orthogonal contrast results of mixed effects model tests for differences in the amount of chemical remaining in decaying stakes incubated above and below-ground for tested harvesting intensification contrasts (FWD = Fine Woody Debris removal, BGBR = below-ground biomass removal, FFR = Forest Floor Removal). Each chemical compound is displayed as text at the effect size based on glm mixed models including a polynomial Mass loss relationship, orthogonal contrasts, and field replicate as a random effect. Error bars display the standard error of the estimated effect size. The treatments in the contrast are displayed on the graph to indicate the direction of effect. Significant effects are indicated with dark grey if they indicate increased values in harvested treatments, contrast-related coloration if they are increased in the reference conditions, and insignificant effects are indicated in light grey."}
Chem_4plot %>% 
      group_by(Contrast, name, position, Estimate, `Std. Error`, `Pr(>|t|)`) %>%
      mutate(Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("TR", "FWD", "BGBR", "FFR","OC", "HF"))) %>%
      filter(Contrast %in% c("FWD", "BGBR", "FFR"), !name %in% c("C", "CNRatio")) %>%
      summarize(groupA = unique(group)[1], 
                groupB = unique(group)[2]) %>% 
      filter(!is.na(Contrast)) %>% 
      mutate(significant = ifelse(`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05, 
                                  ifelse(Estimate > 0, "pos", "neg"), 
                                  "ns")) %>%
      group_by(name) %>% 
      mutate(has.sig = sum(`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05) > 0) %>%
      mutate(position = ifelse(position == "Above-ground", "Above", "Below")) %>%
      mutate(name = str_replace_all(name, c("Aluminum" = "Al", "Boron" = "B", "Calcium" = "Ca", "Copper" = "Cu", "Iron" = "Fe", "Magnesium" = "Mg", "Manganese" = "Mn", "Phosphorus" = "P", "Potassium" = "K", "Zinc" = "Zn", "CNRatio" = "C/N"))) %>%
      mutate(category = ifelse(name %in% c("C", "N", "P", "C/N", "K", "Ca", "Mg"), "macronutrient", ifelse(name %in% c("Fe", "Al", "Cu"), "enzyme biocatalysts", "micronutrient")) ) %>%
      group_by(position, category) %>%
      mutate(groupB.textloc=max(Estimate + `Std. Error`)*1.1, 
             groupA.textloc=min(Estimate - `Std. Error`)*1.1) %>% group_by() %>% 
      mutate(plotting_group = paste(position, Contrast, category), 
             plot_title = paste(Contrast, position)) %>% 
      arrange(category, position, Contrast)-> chem.effect.plot

excluded.vars <- unique(chem.effect.plot$name[!chem.effect.plot$has.sig])

plot_list <- list()
count = 1
for(plot_group in unique(chem.effect.plot$plotting_group)) {
      
      title_text <- chem.effect.plot %>% filter(has.sig, plotting_group == plot_group) %>% summarize(unique(plot_title)) %>% unlist()
      
      plot_panel_colors <- c(TR = "black", FWD = "#44AA99", BGBR = "#CC6677", FFR = "#882235", OC = "#117733", HF = "#AA4499", ML = "black")
      plot_panel_color <- plot_panel_colors[str_extract(title_text, str_c(names(plot_panel_colors), collapse = "|"))]
      
      plot_list[[count]] <- ggplot(chem.effect.plot %>% filter(has.sig, plotting_group == plot_group), aes(1, Estimate,  group = name, color = significant)) + 
      geom_text(aes(label=groupA, y = groupA.textloc), color = "grey30", size = 5) + 
      geom_text(aes(label=groupB, y = groupB.textloc), color = plot_panel_color, size = 5) +
      geom_hline(yintercept=0, color = "grey66", linewidth = 1) +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - `Std. Error`, ymax = Estimate + `Std. Error`), position = position_dodge(width = 1), linewidth =1) +
      geom_label(aes(label = name), position = position_dodge(width = 1)) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position="none", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.title = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey66"), plot.title = element_text(color = plot_panel_color)) + 
      scale_color_manual(values = c("ns"="grey80", "neg"="grey30", "pos" = plot_panel_color[[1]])) + 
            ggtitle(title_text) 
      
      names(plot_list)[count] <- plot_group
      count = count + 1
}
rm(count, title_text)
      
      ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list, labels = "AUTO", ncol = 6, nrow = 3, legend = FALSE) %>% annotate_figure(left ="Estimated effect of contrast on the proportion of chemical amount change", bottom = paste0(str_c(excluded.vars, collapse = ", "), "  were not significantly different in any contrast."))

```




# Supplemental Tables

```{r chemistry-summary-table-AG, cache = F}
#` Table summarizing the range of chemistry measurements, and the number of samples for sister stakes and incubated stakes
chem_DLs <- readRDS("data/chem_DLs.RDS") %>%
      mutate(name = ifelse(parameter == "P", "Phosphorus", parameter),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "K", "Potassium", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Ca", "Calcium", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Mg", "Magnesium", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Fe", "Iron", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Cu", "Copper", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Mn", "Manganese", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Zn", "Zinc", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "S", "Sulfur", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "B", "Boron", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "P", "Phosphorus", name),
             name = ifelse(parameter == "Al", "Aluminum", name), 
             units = "ppm", 
             DL = as.numeric(DL)) 
## year == 0 is Sister_stake
chemistry <- test.set.Harvest$chemistry %>% left_join(chem_DLs, by = "name") %>% 
      filter(!name %in% "CNRatio") %>%
      filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
      mutate(Treatment = factor(ifelse(is.na(StakeID), "Sister Stake", as.character(Treatment)), levels = c("Sister Stake", "OLD", "FIRE", "UC", "SO", "FT", "S", "B")), 
             units = ifelse(is.na(units), "%", units), 
             name = paste0(name, " (", units, ")"), 
             position = ifelse(H.V == "H", "aboveground", "belowground"), 
             position = ifelse(Treatment == "Sister Stake", "", position)) %>%
      group_by(Treatment, name, year, position)  %>% 
      summarize(observations = n(), 
                below_DL = sum(value < DL),
                range = str_c(round(range(value), 2), collapse = "-"), 
                mean = paste0(round(mean(value), 2), " (", round(sd(value), 2), ")")) %>% 
      mutate(observations = ifelse(!is.na(below_DL), paste0(observations, " (", below_DL, " BDL)"), as.character(observations)), 
             table.text = paste(mean, observations, sep = "<br>GAP<br>")) %>% 
      group_by() %>% 
      group_by(Treatment, year, position) %>% 
      dplyr::select(table.text, name) %>%
      pivot_wider(values_from = table.text, names_from = name)
      
knitr::kable(chemistry %>% filter(position %in% c("", "aboveground")), caption = "Wood stake chemistry for stakes incubated above-ground. Values represent the mean and standard deviation as 'mean (sd)' on the first line, and total number of observations, as well as number of observations below detection limits are on the second line as '# (# BDL)'.", format = "pipe", escape = F)      

#flextable::flextable(chemistry %>% filter(position %in% c("", "aboveground")), caption = "Wood stake chemistry for stakes incubated above-ground. Values represent the mean and standard deviation as 'mean (sd)' on the first line, and total number of observations, as well as number of observations below detection limits are on the second line as '# (# BDL)'.")   
```


```{r chemistry-summary-table-BG}
knitr::kable(chemistry %>% filter(position %in% c("", "belowground")), caption = "Wood stake chemistry for stakes incubated below-ground. Values represent the mean and standard deviation as 'mean (sd)' on the first line, and total number of observations, as well as number of observations below detection limits are on the second line as '# (# BDL)'.", format = "pipe", escape = F, cache = F)      
```


# Supplemental Figures

<!--- Significant reductions in density as years progress in both-above ground and below-ground. 
 Above-ground, FFR and FWD had less density losses, while OC effect had more reduction in density. 
 Below-ground TR further decreased density, while FFR, BGBR had increased density compared to the treatments without. --->

<!--- ## Macronutrients --->
```{r carbon, fig.cap = "Patterns of wood stake carbon proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$C
```


```{r nitrogen, fig.cap = "Patterns of wood stake nitrogen proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$N
```


```{r phosphorus, fig.cap = "Patterns of wood stake phosphorus proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Phosphorus
```


```{r calcium, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake calcium proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Calcium
```


```{r potassium, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake potassium proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Potassium
```


```{r magnesium, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake magnesium proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Magnesium
```


<!--- ## Micronutrients --->
```{r manganese, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake manganese proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Manganese
```


```{r boron, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake boron proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Boron
```



```{r zinc, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake zinc proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Zinc
```


<!---## Auxilliary metals--->


```{r aluminum, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake aluminum proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Aluminum
```



```{r copper, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake copper proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Copper
```



```{r iron, fig.cap = "Patterns of above-ground wood stake iron proportion with increased decomposition. Treatments included in Harvesting contrasts (A,C) or Biomass contrasts (B,D) are included for above- (A,B) and below-ground (C,D) wood stakes.  Points representing individual wood stakes and the loess smoothed relationship to mass loss for each treatment are displayed using shape and colour."}
chem_plot_objects$Iron
```
